title: glandularia elegans var. asperata
intro: Glandularia elegans var. asperata, also known as the rough-leafed mock vervain or verbena, is a perennial herb that belongs to the Verbenaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, allowing it to be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Glandularia elegans var. asperata include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and promote the healing of wounds, as well as its potential to help manage anxiety and stress. In herbal preparations, Glandularia elegans var. asperata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to create salves and creams to aid in the healing process.
